question: 1. What is your age?
description: Let's start with some basic information about yourself. This helps us understand who we're serving.

question: 2. How do you identify your gender?
instruct: Choose the option that feels right for you

options.text: Transgender woman (assigned male at birth, identify as woman)
Transgender man (assigned female at birth, identify as man)

question: 3. Who are you attracted to? (Your sexual orientation)
4. Where were you born? (Your native place/birthplace)

options.text: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu

question: 5. Where do you currently live?

options.text: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu

question: 5a. What type of area do you live in?
6. Is your current place of residence the same as your birthplace?
description: These questions help us understand migration patterns within our community.

options.text: Yes, I live where I was born
No, I migrated/moved to a different place

question: 7. If you migrated/moved: When did you come to your current location?
7a. Did you migrate/move alone?

options.text: No, I moved with friends/community members

question: 7b. What was the main reason you migrated to your current location?
8. What is your religion?
9. Do you belong to any of these communities?
instruct: This helps us understand if marginalized caste communities face additional challenges

question: 10. What is the highest level of education you have completed?
11. What do you currently do for work/livelihood?
12. About how much money does your household earn each month?
instruct: (This includes income from all people you live with)

question: 13. Who do you currently live with?

description: The next questions are about your relationships and sexual life. This information helps us provide better sexual health services.
question: 14. Have you ever had sex with someone?
skipLogic: If No skip to Section C

question: 14a. If yes, how old were you when you first had sex?
15. In the last 6 months, about how many people have you had sex with?
16. What is your current relationship situation?
17. When you have anal sex, what role do you usually take?
instruct: You can select more than one

question: 18. Do you have oral sex?
19. In the last 6 months, how often did you or your partner(s) use condoms during anal sex?

options.text: Most of the time (75% or more)
I haven't had anal sex in the last 6 months

question: 20. Have you ever had group sex (sex with 3 or more people at the same time)?
21. Have you ever exchanged sex for money, food, a place to stay, or other things you needed?

options.text: Yes, I do this regularly now (within past month)
Yes, I did this in the past year

skipLogic: If code 4 skip Q21a and Q22

question: 21a. About how many clients do you see in a typical week?
22. In the past month, has any client forced you to have sex without a condom when you wanted to use one?
23. Someone has pushed, shoved, or grabbed me roughly
24. Someone has slapped, hit, kicked, or beat me
25. Someone has choked, strangled, or tried to drown me
26. Someone has attacked me with a weapon (knife, stick, acid, etc.)
27. Someone has burned or scarred me on purpose
28. Who hurt you in these ways?
29. Someone touched me sexually when I didn't want them to
30. Someone made me touch them sexually when I didn't want to
31. Someone made me watch sexual acts or pornography against my will
32. Someone forced me to perform oral sex
33. Someone forced me to have anal or vaginal sex without a condom when I wanted to use one
34. Someone forced me to have anal sex
35. Someone forced me to have vaginal sex
36. Someone put objects or fingers inside me sexually without my consent
37. Someone forced me to participate in sex with multiple people
38. Someone blackmailed or threatened me to get sex
39. Who did this to you?
skipLogic: If ALL Q29-Q38 = No or Prefer not to say, skip

question: 40. After this happened, did you go to a doctor or health facility?
41. Why didn't you seek medical care?

options.text: I didn't think it was serious enough
I was afraid they would blame me
I didn't know where to go
I was afraid my family would find out
I was threatened not to tell anyone
Healthcare staff are discriminatory against LGBTQ+ people

question: 42. Someone has insulted, humiliated, or made fun of me because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
43. Someone has threatened to hurt me or people I care about
44. Someone has threatened to 'out' me (reveal my identity) against my will
45. Someone has isolated me from friends or family
46. Someone has controlled what I do, who I see, or where I go
47. Someone has destroyed my belongings or things that matter to me
48. Someone has used religious or cultural beliefs to make me feel ashamed of who I am
49. Who did this to you?
50. Someone has stopped me from working or earning money
51. Someone has taken my money or income without my permission
52. Someone has controlled my access to money or made all financial decisions for me
53. Someone has refused to give me money for basic needs
54. Someone has denied me inheritance or property rights because of my identity
56. How often does this kind of verbal abuse happen to you?
57. Do you use any dating apps or online platforms to meet sexual or romantic partners?
58. Have you been harassed during the past year more than once while using the platform?
59. Did you report the cyber violence to the platform or authorities?
60. My family has verbally abused me because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
61. My family has physically hurt me because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
62. My family has forced me to marry someone I didn't want to marry
63. My family has forced me to undergo religious rituals or ceremonies to 'change' or 'cure' me
64. My family has forced me to see a doctor or therapist to 'change' or 'cure' me
65. My family has stopped paying for my education because of my identity
66. My family has threatened to disown or cut me off if I don't hide my identity
67. Have you ever been kicked out of your home or forced to leave by your family because of your gender identity or sexual orientation?
68. If you have experienced intimate partner violence, was the perpetrator:
69. How long have you been estranged from your family (if applicable)?
70. I have tried to change my name on legal documents (Aadhaar, passport, PAN card, etc.) to match my affirmed identity
71. When I tried to change my name, I faced difficulties, delays, or was denied
72. I have tried to change my gender marker on legal documents to match my affirmed gender
73. When I tried to change my gender marker, I faced difficulties, delays, or was denied
74. Because of mismatches between my legal documents and my appearance/identity, I avoid situations where I need to show ID
75. I have been questioned, harassed, or denied service because my ID doesn't match my appearance or affirmed identity
76. I was denied admission to a school, college, or educational program because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
77. I was forced to drop out of school or college because of discrimination, harassment, or lack of support related to my identity
78. I was denied use of bathrooms/facilities that match my gender identity in school or college
79. School or college authorities refused to use my affirmed name or correct pronouns
80. I have been denied government welfare benefits or subsidies (ration card, scholarships, pensions, etc.) because of my identity
81. I have been denied services at government offices (passport office, tehsildar office, municipality, etc.) due to discrimination
82. I have been mistreated, harassed, or asked to pay bribes by government officials because of my identity
83. I have faced difficulties accessing legal services or representation in courts due to my identity
84. I have been unable to open a bank account or faced difficulties with banking services due to ID issues or discrimination
85. I have been denied voting or faced difficulties voting because my ID doesn't match my appearance
86. I am unable to legally marry the person I want to because same-sex marriage is not recognized in India
87. Overall, I feel that laws and government policies in India do not protect my rights as an LGBTQ+ person
88. Have you ever used any of the following substances? (Check all that apply)
89. In the past 6 months, how frequently have you used any substances (other than alcohol or tobacco)?
90. Have you ever used drugs specifically to initiate, enhance, or prolong sexual activity? (Do not include alcohol, tobacco, or cannabis)
90a. What substances do you most commonly use for sex? (Check top 3)
91. Have you ever injected drugs (using a needle/syringe)?
91a. What substances have you injected? (Check all that apply)
92. Have you ever been tested for Hepatitis C?
93. Do you drink alcohol?

options.text: No (skip to Section E - Mental Health)

question: 93a. How often do you drink alcohol?
93b. On a typical day when you drink, how many drinks do you have?
93c. How often do you have 6 or more drinks on one occasion?

description: Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?
question: 94. Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
95. Not being able to stop or control worrying
96. Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
97. Little interest or pleasure in doing things
98. If yes, what diagnosis?
99. Are you currently taking any medications for mental health?
100. When facing problems, what do you typically do? (Check all that apply)
101. Have you ever accessed healthcare services (doctor, clinic, hospital)?
101a. When was your last healthcare visit?
101b. Where do you usually go for healthcare?
101c. Have you disclosed your sexual orientation or gender identity to healthcare providers?
101d. Do you have health insurance or any health coverage?
102. What are the main barriers to accessing healthcare? (Check all that apply)
103. Have you ever been tested for HIV?
103b. What was the result of your most recent HIV test?
105. Have you heard of PrEP (Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis)?

options.text: Yes, I understand what it is

question: 105a. Are you currently taking PrEP?
106. Are you currently using hormones for gender affirmation?
107. Does your family know about your sexual orientation or gender identity?
108. How many close friends do you have that you can confide in?
112. Are you aware that Section 377 was struck down in 2018?
114. Are you aware of any organizations or services that provide support to LGBTQ+ people?
118. Have you ever contacted a helpline when facing problems?

options.text: No, but I know helpline numbers
No, I don't know any helpline numbers

question: 119. Have you ever stayed at Garima Grih or any shelter/safe house?

options.text: No, but I know about it

question: 120. Have you ever received support from a Community-Based Organization (CBO)?

options.text: No, but I know about CBOs
No, I don't know what CBOs are

question: 121. In your hijra community, how often do you seek guidance from your guru for personal problems?

options.text: Always - my guru is my first support
Never - I don't seek guru's guidance
Not applicable - not part of hijra community

question: 122. Would you be interested in receiving training or capacity building support?
123. What type of training would be most helpful? (Select up to 5)

options.text: Resume writing and job interview skills

question: 124. What format of training would work best? (Select all)
126. Would you be willing to train others if you received training?
132. When pursuing gender-affirming services what did you hope to achieve?

options.text: Be recognized in my true gender

question: 137. Do you consider aging transgender individuals vulnerable?
140. Because of extreme weather or environmental problems I have had trouble accessing sexual health services.
141. In the past 12 months, extreme heat has affected my daily life, work, or health.
142. In the past 12 months, flooding, heavy rains, or water-related disasters have affected me or my community.
143. Climate change or environmental problems have made it harder for me to access healthcare, medications, or health services.
144. Environmental or climate problems have increased stress, anxiety, or other mental health challenges for me.
145. I have had to move or migrate because of environmental problems, droughts, floods, or climate-related reasons.
146. During extreme weather or disasters, I faced discrimination or was denied help because of my gender identity or sexual orientation.
147. I worry about how climate change or environmental problems will affect my life and safety in the future.
148. Would you be interested in receiving health services through a virtual platform?
149. Do you feel comfortable reaching out to a health professional using online tools or your phone?
150. Which method would you prefer to connect with a health worker virtually?
151a. It would feel more private or confidential.
151b. It would be easier to access from where I live.
151c. It would feel less stigmatising or judgement-free.
151d. It would save me time or money on travel.
151e. I would feel safer being open about my identity or health status.
151f. I would be able to get results or information faster.
151g. It would make it easier to follow up on my care or treatment.
152. Is there any other important topic we didn't ask about?

options.text: Issues related to aging as LGBTQ+ person
Experiences with police or legal system

question: 153. How did you feel about this interview?
154. Would you like a referral to any services?
